text.title: javascript-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for javascript-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Testing implementation details: checking internal state (useState values), spying on private methods, or asserting on mock call counts without asserting the output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Implementation-detail tests break on every refactor, even if behavior unchanged. Test observable behavior (return value, side effect) instead. If a refactor changes internals without changing behavior, tests should still pass.

failure-mode.description: Using getByTestId for everything to avoid learning role/label queries; testing component props directly instead of user interactions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: testId queries are brittle (tied to DOM structure). Role/label queries verify accessibility and match user-visible names. Direct props are implementation — users don't see props, they see rendered output.

failure-mode.description: Forking mocks per test type (one set for unit tests, one for Storybook, one for Playwright) or embedding mock data directly in test files.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forks diverge and become inconsistent, leading to tests that pass locally but fail in CI (production behavior unmodeled). Centralized MSW handlers are the single source of truth; all test types use the same mocks.
